text.title: ES Agentic Pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules an AI agent (and its reviewer) MUST satisfy when scaffolding event-sourced code.

rule.statement: Event names MUST be past-tense facts (`OrderPlaced`, `PaymentCaptured`, `ItemAdded`). Names like `UpdateOrder`, `OrderUpdate`, `OrderUpdated_v2` are forbidden — they encode CRUD shape, not business intent.

rationale.source: EventStorming / Greg Young — Event Sourcing primer

rule.rationale: CRUD-shaped events erase intent and break downstream projections that depend on specific business meanings.

rule.statement: Aggregate state MUST be mutated only inside `apply` / `_apply` event-handlers. Command methods (`place`, `cancel`) MUST validate, emit events, and return — they MUST NOT touch `self.status` or any state field directly.
rule.rationale: Mutation in command methods makes replay diverge from live execution; rebuilding state from events produces a different result.

rule.statement: Repository `save(stream_id, events, expected_version)` MUST always pass the version loaded at command start. The event store MUST raise `ConcurrencyError` when versions disagree. Omitting `expected_version` is a P0 review reject.
rule.rationale: Without expected_version, concurrent commands silently clobber each other; reconstructed state is wrong.

rule.statement: Generated code MUST call APIs that exist in the target event-store library (verified by import-resolution). Agents hallucinating `event_store.append_one(...)`, `repo.put_events(...)`, `bus.fire(...)` MUST be rejected.

rationale.source: LLM hallucination patterns + CI build failures

rule.rationale: Hallucinated APIs compile-fail or runtime-fail; even if both happen, downstream cost dominates.

rule.statement: Projection handlers MUST only write to the read store (UPSERT / INSERT / DELETE). They MUST NOT send email, call other services, raise domain events, or run business logic. Side effects belong in process managers / sagas.
rule.rationale: Projections rebuild from offset 0 periodically; side effects would fire each rebuild, producing duplicate emails / charges / events.
